summary: Iteration 8 testing: RBAC enforcement on commercial pipeline endpoints, Live Documents inbox/diff endpoints, Stability scheduler endpoints, RoleGuard frontend wrapper, TasksPage 'Rodar scan D-2' button, and PDDetail 'Documentos Vivos' tab. Backend: 29 tests pass + 1 xfail (real RBAC bug found on /api/pd/requests). Frontend: 14/14 RBAC route checks pass; scan D-2 button visible+functional for admin/qa, hidden for vendedor; Live Documents tab renders empty state with FT/EPA toggles and reviewer 'Gerar versão' button.

critical.issue: RBAC NOT enforced. Vendedor can list PD requests (returns 200) although PD_READ does NOT include vendedor. server.py wraps commercial pipeline routes with get_commercial_user, but the symmetric protection on PD endpoints is missing in pd_routes.py. Code in /app/backend/pd_routes.py:1104 defines list_pd_requests without require_roles call. This is the 'fallback scenario' explicitly called out in the test request.

action_items: Add require_roles(user, PD_READ) to /api/pd/requests GET endpoint (and verify other pd_routes endpoints follow the same pattern). Currently any authenticated user (including vendedor) can list/read PD requests, which violates RBAC matrix where vendedor is not in PD_READ.
Optional: confirm whether vendedor should see /pd/requests/{id} & PD details via UI — RoleGuard already blocks /pd routes on the frontend, but backend should still enforce.
critical_code_review_comments: Inconsistent RBAC enforcement: server.py systematically applies get_commercial_user() to commercial pipeline routes (good), but pd_routes.py only uses require_roles() on selected endpoints (homologacao, scheduler, etc). The base /requests CRUD endpoints lack role checks - any authenticated tenant member, including vendedor, can read PD data. Recommend adding a get_pd_user/get_pd_read_user helper and applying consistently.
Live documents diff endpoint currently lacks explicit role check; relies on _get_live_document_version (need to verify it scopes by tenant + role). Consider adding require_roles(user, DOC_REVIEWERS).
Stability scheduler endpoints correctly enforce roles (PD_READ|DOC_REVIEWERS for status; ADMIN_ONLY|{liderpd,qa,formulador} for run-scheduler). Both verified.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (RBAC route guards, scan D-2 visibility, Live Docs tab elements all verified)

seed_data_creation: Created 1 PD request 'TEST_ITER8_LiveDocs' (id 0d56dcb1-2937-46ba-9817-00f1c7a2efd4) in admin tenant for testing PDDetail Live Documents tab. Also created 1 commercial card 'TEST_ITER8_RBAC_OK' via vendedor session.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/test_iter8_rbac_livedocs.py - 29 pass + 1 xfail (the xfail will flip to PASS once main agent adds require_roles(PD_READ) to /api/pd/requests). Auth uses HttpOnly cookies. Live Documents tab currently shows empty state since no FT/EPA versions are auto-generated for fresh PD requests; toggles FT<->EPA work, 'Gerar versão' button visible to qa (DOC_REVIEWERS). 'Rodar scan D-2' button confirmed visible+working for admin/qa/formulador/liderpd, hidden for vendedor/salesops. RoleGuard 'Acesso restrito' screen working correctly across all 4 tested roles.
rca_of_issue: Bug 1 (RBAC gap on /api/pd/requests): pd_routes.py:1104 list_pd_requests only calls get_current_user, not require_roles. Mitigation: add `require_roles(user, PD_READ)` after get_current_user. Same review needed for other /requests CRUD handlers and any other /pd/* endpoint without explicit role check.
